questions.q: What was the name of the film produced by Dadasaheb Torne?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Torne produced 'Shree Pundalik', screened in Bombay in 1912.

questions.q: In which year was Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' screened in Bombay?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was screened in Bombay on 18 May 1912.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne's full name was Ramchandra Gopal Torne. By what nickname is he commonly known?
questions.explanation: Ramchandra Gopal Torne is commonly known by the honorific 'Dadasaheb'.

questions.q: On which date in 1912 was Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' publicly screened in Bombay?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was publicly screened on 18 May 1912.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' predated which landmark 1913 film by roughly a year?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' (1912) preceded Dadasaheb Phalke's Raja Harishchandra (1913) by about a year.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' was based on what kind of source material?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was a recording of a Marathi stage play rather than an original cinematic narrative.

questions.q: In which city was Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' first publicly screened?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was first publicly screened in Bombay (Mumbai).

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne is best remembered as the recorder and producer of which early Indian film?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Torne is remembered as the producer of 'Shree Pundalik'.

questions.q: The legend of Pundalik in 'Shree Pundalik' is associated with the worship of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Pundalik legend is central to the devotion of Lord Vitthal (Vithoba) of Pandharpur.

questions.q: A long-running debate surrounds Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' concerning which question?
options: Whether it was India's first colour film
Whether it qualifies as India's first film
Whether it was India's first talkie
Whether it was India's first animated film
questions.explanation: Historians debate whether 'Shree Pundalik' qualifies as the first Indian film.

questions.q: Why do some historians hesitate to call Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' the first Indian film?
options: It was never shown to the public
It was a recorded stage play shot by a British cameraman
questions.explanation: Critics note it was a recorded stage play filmed by a British cameraman, not an originally conceived motion picture.

questions.q: In what year was Dadasaheb Torne born?
questions.explanation: Ramchandra Gopal 'Dadasaheb' Torne was born in 1890.

questions.q: In what year did Dadasaheb Torne die?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Torne died on 19 January 1960.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' run as a film?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was roughly 1,500 feet of film, running for about 22 minutes.

questions.q: At which Bombay venue was Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' screened in 1912?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was screened at the Coronation Cinematograph in Girgaum, Bombay.

questions.q: Which pioneer's Raja Harishchandra is widely cited as India's first feature film, ahead of Dadasaheb Torne's claim?
questions.explanation: Dhundiraj Govind 'Dadasaheb' Phalke's Raja Harishchandra (1913) is widely cited as India's first feature film.

questions.q: The cameraman who filmed Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' was of which nationality?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was shot by a British cameraman named Johnson.

questions.q: In which Indian region's film industry was Dadasaheb Torne later professionally active?
options: The Marathi and Bombay film industry
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Torne worked within the Marathi and Bombay-based film industry.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne later founded which film production company in Pune?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Torne founded Saraswati Cinetone, a film studio based in Pune.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne is credited with supplying and operating the sound equipment for which 1931 film, India's first talkie?
questions.explanation: Torne acquired the talkie machinery and helped Ardeshir Irani's Imperial Film Company produce 'Alam Ara' (1931), India's first sound film.

questions.q: Roughly how long before Phalke's Raja Harishchandra was Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' screened?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' (May 1912) preceded Raja Harishchandra (1913) by roughly a year.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' was a film in which language tradition of theatre?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' was based on a Marathi stage play.

questions.q: A key argument against crediting Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' as the first Indian film is that, unlike Raja Harishchandra, it lacked which element?
options: Original cinematic direction and editing rather than a filmed play
questions.explanation: Scholars argue 'Shree Pundalik' was merely a filmed stage performance, lacking the original cinematic craft of Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' was reportedly processed and developed using facilities in which country?
questions.explanation: The negatives of 'Shree Pundalik' are reported to have been sent to London for processing.

questions.q: By what name is the British cameraman who shot Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' commonly identified?
questions.explanation: The cameraman of 'Shree Pundalik' is commonly identified as a British national named Johnson.

questions.q: Saraswati Cinetone, the studio founded by Dadasaheb Torne, was based in which city?
questions.explanation: Saraswati Cinetone, founded by Dadasaheb Torne, was based in Pune (near Shankarsheth Road).

questions.q: What is the central act of devotion in the Pundalik legend depicted in Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik'?
options: Pundalik renouncing the world as an ascetic
Pundalik's devoted service to his parents
questions.explanation: The Pundalik legend centres on his devoted service to his aged parents, prompting Vitthal to wait on a brick for him.

questions.q: After its 1912 premiere in Bombay, how long is Dadasaheb Torne's 'Shree Pundalik' reported to have run?
questions.explanation: 'Shree Pundalik' is reported to have run for about two weeks at the Coronation Cinematograph in Bombay.

questions.q: Compared with Raja Harishchandra, which point is often used to support, rather than dismiss, Dadasaheb Torne's priority claim for 'Shree Pundalik'?
options: Its public screening simply occurred earlier, in May 1912
It was the first film with synchronized sound
It was longer in running time
It won an early international award
questions.explanation: Supporters of Torne's claim emphasize that 'Shree Pundalik' was publicly screened in May 1912, earlier than Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: Within Indian cinema history, Dadasaheb Torne is best described as which kind of figure?
options: A celebrated singing star of the 1930s
A contested pioneer whose 1912 film rivals the standard origin story
The first official film censor of India
questions.explanation: Torne is regarded as a contested pioneer whose 1912 'Shree Pundalik' challenges the conventional dating of Indian cinema's beginnings.
